text: I couldn't get an appointment for almost three weeks, and even then the clinic was nowhere near a bus line, so I had to take a whole day off work just to be seen.
My copay went up again this year, so I've quietly been skipping the follow-up visits even though I know I really shouldn't be doing that.
The front desk staff were dismissive when I asked about the sliding-scale fee, like the cost of care was something I should already understand.
The doctor actually explained everything clearly and I felt listened to for the first time in years, which honestly surprised me after so many rushed visits.
Getting a referral to the specialist took forever, and nobody warned me the wait list was four months long until I called to check.
I trust my nurse practitioner more than any physician I've seen; she remembers my history and never makes me feel like a number.
The patient portal is confusing, so I gave up on booking online and just call, but then I sit on hold for half an hour every single time.
When my insurance changed, the billing office sent me to three different numbers before anyone could tell me whether the visit was even covered.
There's no transit out here after six, so evening appointments are impossible unless a neighbor can drive me, and most of them work late too.
Honestly the telehealth option was a lifesaver during the snow, but the connection kept dropping and we had to restart the call twice.
